Executes a given bash command in a persistent shell session with optional timeout, ensuring proper handling and security measures.

Be aware: OS: linux, Shell: bash

All commands run in the current working directory by default. Use the `workdir` parameter if you need to run a command in a different directory. AVOID using `cd <directory> && <command>` patterns - use `workdir` instead.

Use `/tmp/opencode` for temporary work outside the workspace. This directory has already been created, already exists, and is pre-approved for external directory access.

IMPORTANT: This tool is for terminal operations like git, npm, docker, etc. DO NOT use it for file operations (reading, writing, editing, searching, finding files) - use the specialized tools for this instead.

Before executing the command, please follow these steps:

1. Directory Verification:
   - If the command will create new directories or files, first use `ls` to verify the parent directory exists and is the correct location
   - For example, before running "mkdir foo/bar", first use `ls foo` to check that "foo" exists and is the intended parent directory

2. Command Execution:
   - Always quote file paths that contain spaces with double quotes (e.g., rm "path with spaces/file.txt")
   - Examples of proper quoting:
     - mkdir "/Users/name/My Documents" (correct)
     - mkdir /Users/name/My Documents (incorrect - will fail)
     - python "/path/with spaces/script.py" (correct)
     - python /path/with spaces/script.py (incorrect - will fail)
   - After ensuring proper quoting, execute the command.
   - Capture the output of the command.

Usage notes:
  - The command argument is required.
  - You can specify an optional timeout in milliseconds. If not specified, commands will time out after 120000ms (2 minutes).
  - It is very helpful if you write a clear, concise description of what this command does in 5-10 words.
  - If the output exceeds 2000 lines or 51200 bytes, it will be truncated and the full output will be written to a file. You can use Read with offset/limit to read specific sections or Grep to search the full content. Do NOT use `head`, `tail`, or other truncation commands to limit output; the full output will already be captured to a file for more precise searching.

  - Avoid using Bash with the `find`, `grep`, `cat`, `head`, `tail`, `sed`, `awk`, or `echo` commands, unless explicitly instructed or when these commands are truly necessary for the task. Instead, always prefer using the dedicated tools for these commands:
    - File search: Use Glob (NOT find or ls)
    - Content search: Use Grep (NOT grep or rg)
    - Read files: Use Read (NOT cat/head/tail)
    - Edit files: Use Edit (NOT sed/awk)
    - Write files: Use Write (NOT echo >/cat <<EOF)
    - Communication: Output text directly (NOT echo/printf)
  - When issuing multiple commands:
    - If the commands are independent and can run in parallel, make multiple Bash tool calls in a single message. For example, if you need to run "git status" and "git diff", send a single message with two Bash tool calls in parallel.
    - If the commands depend on each other and must run sequentially, use a single Bash call with '&&' to chain them together (e.g., `git add . && git commit -m "message" && git push`). For instance, if one operation must complete before another starts (like mkdir before cp, Write before Bash for git operations, or git add before git commit), run these operations sequentially instead.
    - Use ';' only when you need to run commands sequentially but don't care if earlier commands fail
    - DO NOT use newlines to separate commands (newlines are ok in quoted strings)
  - AVOID using `cd <directory> && <command>`. Use the `workdir` parameter to change directories instead.
    <good-example>
    Use workdir="/foo/bar" with command: pytest tests
    </good-example>
    <bad-example>
    cd /foo/bar && pytest tests

# Committing changes with git

Only create commits when requested by the user. If unclear, ask first. When the user asks you to create a new git commit, follow these steps carefully:

Git Safety Protocol:
- NEVER update the git config
- NEVER run destructive/irreversible git commands (like push --force, hard reset, etc) unless the user explicitly requests them
- NEVER skip hooks (--no-verify, --no-gpg-sign, etc) unless the user explicitly requests it
- NEVER run force push to main/master, warn the user if they request it
- Avoid git commit --amend. ONLY use --amend when ALL conditions are met:
  (1) User explicitly requested amend, OR the commit succeeded and pre-commit hooks auto-modified files that need including — verify by checking `git log` that HEAD is the new commit before amending
  (2) HEAD commit was created by you in this conversation (verify: git log -1 --format='%an %ae')
  (3) Commit has NOT been pushed to remote (verify: git status shows "Your branch is ahead")
- CRITICAL: If commit FAILED or was REJECTED by hook, NEVER amend - fix the issue and create a NEW commit
- CRITICAL: If you already pushed to remote, NEVER amend unless user explicitly requests it (requires force push)
- NEVER commit changes unless the user explicitly asks you to. It is VERY IMPORTANT to only commit when explicitly asked, otherwise the user will feel that you are being too proactive.

1. You can call multiple tools in a single response. When multiple independent pieces of information are requested and all commands are likely to succeed, run multiple tool calls in parallel for optimal performance. run the following bash commands in parallel, each using the Bash tool:
  - Run a git status command to see all untracked files.
  - Run a git diff command to see both staged and unstaged changes that will be committed.
  - Run a git log command to see recent commit messages, so that you can follow this repository's commit message style.
2. Analyze all staged changes (both previously staged and newly added) and draft a commit message:
  - Summarize the nature of the changes (eg. new feature, enhancement to an existing feature, bug fix, refactoring, test, docs, etc.). Ensure the message accurately reflects the changes and their purpose (i.e. "add" means a wholly new feature, "update" means an enhancement to an existing feature, "fix" means a bug fix, etc.).
  - Do not commit files that likely contain secrets (.env, credentials.json, etc.). Warn the user if they specifically request to commit those files
  - Draft a concise (1-2 sentences) commit message that focuses on the "why" rather than the "what"
  - Ensure it accurately reflects the changes and their purpose
3. You can call multiple tools in a single response. When multiple independent pieces of information are requested and all commands are likely to succeed, run multiple tool calls in parallel for optimal performance. run the following commands:
   - Add relevant untracked files to the staging area.
   - Create the commit with a message
   - Run git status after the commit completes to verify success.
   Note: git status depends on the commit completing, so run it sequentially after the commit.
4. If the commit fails due to pre-commit hook, fix the issue and create a NEW commit (see amend rules above)

Important notes:
- NEVER run additional commands to read or explore code, besides git bash commands
- NEVER use the TodoWrite or Task tools
- DO NOT push to the remote repository unless the user explicitly asks you to do so
- IMPORTANT: Never use git commands with the -i flag (like git rebase -i or git add -i) since they require interactive input which is not supported.
- If there are no changes to commit (i.e., no untracked files and no modifications), do not create an empty commit

2. Analyze all changes that will be included in the pull request, making sure to look at all relevant commits (NOT just the latest commit, but ALL commits that will be included in the pull request!!!), and draft a pull request summary
3. You can call multiple tools in a single response. When multiple independent pieces of information are requested and all commands are likely to succeed, run multiple tool calls in parallel for optimal performance. run the following commands in parallel:

Use this tool to create and manage a structured task list for your current coding session. This helps you track progress, organize complex tasks, and demonstrate thoroughness to the user.
It also helps the user understand the progress of the task and overall progress of their requests.

## When to Use This Tool
Use this tool proactively in these scenarios:

1. Complex multistep tasks - When a task requires 3 or more distinct steps or actions
2. Non-trivial and complex tasks - Tasks that require careful planning or multiple operations
3. User explicitly requests todo list - When the user directly asks you to use the todo list
4. User provides multiple tasks - When users provide a list of things to be done (numbered or comma-separated)
5. After receiving new instructions - Immediately capture user requirements as todos. Feel free to edit the todo list based on new information.
6. After completing a task - Mark it complete and add any new follow-up tasks
7. When you start working on a new task, mark the todo as in_progress. Ideally you should only have one todo as in_progress at a time. Complete existing tasks before starting new ones.

## When NOT to Use This Tool

Skip using this tool when:
1. There is only a single, straightforward task
2. The task is trivial and tracking it provides no organizational benefit
3. The task can be completed in less than 3 trivial steps
4. The task is purely conversational or informational

NOTE that you should not use this tool if there is only one trivial task to do. In this case you are better off just doing the task directly.

## Examples of When to Use the Todo List

<example>
User: I want to add a dark mode toggle to the application settings. Make sure you run the tests and build when you're done!
Assistant: I'll help add a dark mode toggle to your application settings. Let me create a todo list to track this implementation.
*Creates todo list with the following items:*
1. Create dark mode toggle component in Settings page
2. Add dark mode state management (context/store)
3. Implement CSS-in-JS styles for dark theme
4. Update existing components to support theme switching
5. Run tests and build process, addressing any failures or errors that occur
*Begins working on the first task*

<reasoning>
The assistant used the todo list because:
1. Adding dark mode is a multi-step feature requiring UI, state management, and styling changes
2. The user explicitly requested tests and build be run afterward
3. The assistant inferred that tests and build need to pass by adding "Ensure tests and build succeed" as the final task
</reasoning>
</example>

<example>
User: Help me rename the function getCwd to getCurrentWorkingDirectory across my project
Assistant: Let me first search through your codebase to find all occurrences of 'getCwd'.
*Uses grep or search tools to locate all instances of getCwd in the codebase*
Assistant: I've found 15 instances of 'getCwd' across 8 different files. Let me create a todo list to track these changes.
*Creates todo list with specific items for each file that needs updating*

<reasoning>
The assistant used the todo list because:
1. First, the assistant searched to understand the scope of the task
2. Upon finding multiple occurrences across different files, it determined this was a complex task with multiple steps
3. The todo list helps ensure every instance is tracked and updated systematically
4. This approach prevents missing any occurrences and maintains code consistency
</reasoning>
</example>


<example>
User: I need to implement these features for my e-commerce site: user registration, product catalog, shopping cart, and checkout flow.
Assistant: I'll help implement these features. First, let's add all the features to the todo list.
*Creates a todo list breaking down each feature into specific tasks based on the project architecture*
Assistant: Let's start with user registration. This will involve creating database models, API endpoints, and frontend forms that integrate with your existing authentication system.

<reasoning>
The assistant used the todo list because:
1. The user provided multiple complex features to implement in a comma separated list
2. The todo list helps organize these large features into manageable tasks
3. This approach allows for tracking progress across the entire implementation
</reasoning>
</example>

<example>
User: Can you help optimize my React application? It's rendering slowly and has performance issues.</user>
Assistant: I'll help optimize your React application. First, let me examine your codebase to identify potential performance bottlenecks.
*Reviews component structure, render patterns, state management, and data fetching*
Assistant: After analyzing your codebase, I've identified several performance issues. Let me create a todo list to track our optimization efforts.
*Creates todo list with items like: 1) Implement memoization for expensive calculations in ProductList, 2) Add virtualization for long lists in Dashboard, 3) Optimize image loading in Gallery component, 4) Fix state update loops in ShoppingCart, 5) Review bundle size and implement code splitting*
Let's start by implementing memoization for the expensive calculations in your ProductList component.</assistant>

<reasoning>
The assistant used the todo list because:
1. First, the assistant examined the codebase to identify specific performance issues
2. Based on this analysis, it identified multiple optimization opportunities
3. Performance optimization is a non-trivial task requiring multiple steps
4. The todo list helps methodically track improvements across different components
5. This systematic approach ensures all performance bottlenecks are addressed
</reasoning>
</example>

## Examples of When NOT to Use the Todo List

<example>
User: How do I print 'Hello World' in Python?
Assistant: In Python, you can print "Hello World" with this simple code:

python
print("Hello World")

This will output the text "Hello World" to the console when executed.</assistant>

<reasoning>
The assistant did not use the todo list because this is a single, trivial task that can be completed in one step. There's no need to track multiple tasks or steps for such a straightforward request.
</reasoning>
</example>

<example>
User: What does the git status command do?
Assistant: The git status command shows the current state of your working directory and staging area. It displays which changes have been staged, which haven't, and which files aren't being tracked by Git. This is a fundamental command for understanding the current state of your repository before making commits.

<reasoning>
The assistant did not use the todo list because this is an informational request with no actual coding task to complete. The user is simply asking for an explanation, not for the assistant to perform multiple steps or tasks.
</reasoning>
</example>

<example>
User: Can you add a comment to the calculateTotal function to explain what it does?
Assistant: Sure, let me add a comment to the calculateTotal function to explain what it does.
* Uses the Edit tool to add a comment to the calculateTotal function *

<reasoning>
The assistant did not use the todo list because this is a single, straightforward task confined to one location in the code. Adding a comment doesn't require tracking multiple steps or systematic organization.

Assistant: I'll run the npm install command for you.

The command completed successfully. Here's the output:

All dependencies have been installed according to your package.json file.

<reasoning>
The assistant did not use the todo list because this is a single command execution with immediate results. There are no multiple steps to track or organize, making the todo list unnecessary for this straightforward task.
</reasoning>
</example>

## Task States and Management

1. **Task States**: Use these states to track progress:
   - pending: Task not yet started
   - in_progress: Currently working on (limit to ONE task at a time)
   - completed: Task finished successfully
   - cancelled: Task no longer needed

2. **Task Management**:
   - Update task status in real-time as you work
   - Mark tasks complete IMMEDIATELY after finishing (don't batch completions)
   - Only have ONE task in_progress at any time
   - Complete current tasks before starting new ones
   - Cancel tasks that become irrelevant

3. **Task Breakdown**:
   - Create specific, actionable items
   - Break complex tasks into smaller, manageable steps
   - Use clear, descriptive task names

When in doubt, use this tool. Being proactive with task management demonstrates attentiveness and ensures you complete all requirements successfully.

data.content:
# Write Cypher

## Overview

Use this skill for VAST DB Cypher query patterns. Prefer reusable templates, and construct new queries when no template matches the analysis need.

## Workflow

1. Read `references/query-templates.md`. If a template matches the task, reuse it as-is.
2. If no template matches, construct a new Cypher query using the VAST DB schema and the closest related template as guidance.

## Query Strategy

- Load `understand-mlir-schema` when query construction or result interpretation depends on schema details.
- Use small exploratory queries with `LIMIT` when schema details such as operation names, properties, node and relationship labels are uncertain.
- When starting from a source location, first map it to operation `uid`s. Use those `uid`s as stable handles for follow-up queries such as enclosing functions, call relationships, and def-use data flow.
- Return only properties needed for the analysis. Common fields are node `uid`, relationship `idx`, operation `name`, `location`, `func_name`, `fs_raw_name`, `varName`, `className`, type `type`, and attribute `value`.
- Order indexed relationships by `idx`.

## Query Rules

- Generate read-only queries by default. Do not use `CREATE`, `MERGE`, `SET`, `DELETE`, or `REMOVE`.
- Use parameter placeholders such as `$func_name`, `$op_uid`, `$file_name`, `$line`, and `$callee`.
- Do not invent node and relationship labels, properties, or operation names. Verify unfamiliar schema details with `understand-mlir-schema` or exploratory queries.

## Common Patterns

Read `references/query-templates.md` for reusable Cypher templates.

Use this before writing Cypher queries against VAST DB. For query patterns, load write-cypher.

# Understand MLIR Schema

## Overview

This skill describes the VAST DB schema. Use it to check node labels, relationship types, and properties before writing or interpreting Cypher queries. Do not guess labels or relationships; look them up here.

## Core Schema

### Node Labels

- `OPERATIONS` — MLIR operations. Properties include `uid`, `name`, `func_name`, `fs_raw_name`, `varName`, `mod_name`, and `location`. The `location` field has the form `['F', file, line, column]`.
- `VALUES` — SSA values. Only property is `uid`.
- `INTATTRS` — Integer attributes. Properties: `uid`, `value` (int), `attr_type` (int).
- `FLOATATTRS` — Float attributes. Properties: `uid`, `value` (float), `attr_type` (int).
- `STRINGATTRS` — String attributes. Properties: `uid`, `value` (string), `attr_type` (int).
- `TYPES` — MLIR types. Properties: `uid`, `type` (type mnemonic), `has_dl` (bool), `bw` (int bitwidth), `align` (int), `record_name` (string, for hl.record only).
- `REGIONS` — MLIR regions. Properties: `uid`, `regType` (bool: true for GraphRegion, false for SSACFG).
- `BLOCKS` — MLIR blocks. Only property is `uid`.
- `VTABLES` — Virtual tables. Properties: `uid`, `vtable_name`, `components_flat`, `components_sizes`.
- `VTTS` — VTT (virtual table table). Properties: `uid`, `vtt_name`, `components_flat`.

### Key Relationships

Operation operands and results:
- `(:OPERATIONS)-[:ARGVALUE {idx}]->(:VALUES)` — operation consumes a value as operand
- `(:OPERATIONS)-[:RESVALUE {idx}]->(:VALUES)` — operation produces a value as result
- `(:OPERATIONS)-[:ARGATTR {idx}]->(:INTATTRS|FLOATATTRS|STRINGATTRS)` — operation has an attribute
- `(:OPERATIONS)-[:ARGBLOCK {idx}]->(:BLOCKS)` — operation references a block (e.g., branch targets)
- `(:OPERATIONS)-[:ARGTYPE {idx}]->(:TYPES)` — operation has a type operand

Containment (operation bodies):
- `(:OPERATIONS)-[:CHILDREGION {idx}]->(:REGIONS)` — operation contains a region
- `(:REGIONS)-[:CHILDBLOCK {idx}]->(:BLOCKS)` — region contains a block
- `(:BLOCKS)-[:CHILDINST {idx}]->(:OPERATIONS)` — block contains an instruction (operation)
- `(:BLOCKS)-[:BLOCKARG {idx}]->(:VALUES)` — block has a block argument

Type system:
- `(:VALUES)-[:TYPE]->(:TYPES)` — value has a type
- `(:TYPES)-[:ARG {idx}]->(:TYPES)` — composite type has component types
- `(:INTATTRS|:FLOATATTRS)-[:TYPE]->(:TYPES)` — non-string attribute has a type

Function and variable types:
- `(:OPERATIONS)-[:FUNCTYPE]->(:TYPES)` — global function's type signature
- `(:OPERATIONS)-[:VARTYPE]->(:TYPES)` — global variable's type

Virtual tables (only from `builtin.module` operations):
- `(:OPERATIONS)-[:ARGVTABLE]->(:VTABLES)` — module has a vtable
- `(:OPERATIONS)-[:ARGVTT]->(:VTTS)` — module has a VTT

## References

- `references/schema-mlir-graph.md` — canonical node labels, relationships, properties, constraints, and example queries.
- `references/operations-catalog.md` — every operation name (`hl.func`, `hl.call`, `ll.load`, etc.) with its specific properties and relationships.
- `references/types-catalog.md` — every type mnemonic (`hl.int`, `i32`, `core.fn`, etc.) with its properties and subtype structure.

parts.text:
Both are compiled as object files. The test will compile these with a test harness. The key difference between bad and good is just the NULL check addition on lines 510-511 and 522-523.

Let me now consolidate my judgement.

The fix is on two lines:
1. Line 510-511 (bad: `!*newsum->md5 || !*oldsum->md5` → good: `newsum->md5 == NULL || newsum->md5[0] == '\0' || oldsum->md5 == NULL || oldsum->md5[0] == '\0'`)
2. Line 522-523 (bad: `!*newsum->sha1 || !*oldsum->sha1` → good: `newsum->sha1 == NULL || newsum->sha1[0] == '\0' || oldsum->sha1 == NULL || oldsum->sha1[0] == '\0'`)

The null pointer dereference occurs when `sk_decode_sum` succeeds but leaves pointer fields like `md5` or `sha1` as NULL (because the checksum encoding doesn't contain those fields), and then `fim_alert` dereferences them at line 510/521 without null check.

Now for my judgement of each explanation:

**Baseline:**
- Correctly identifies null pointer dereference (CWE-476)
- Correctly identifies the sink location (line 510)
- Correctly describes that memset sets pointers to NULL
- BUT: Describes the trigger as simply "memset zeros → fim_alert is called → crash", ignoring that `sk_decode_sum` runs between memset and fim_alert. The explanation is incomplete/inaccurate because it doesn't account for the fact that `oldsum` and `newsum` are populated between the memset and the sink.
- Score: 5/10 - identifies the right code but has an oversimplified/incomplete trigger path

**VastDB:**
- Correctly identifies null pointer dereference
- Correctly identifies the sink location
- Correctly notes that memset sets pointers to NULL
- Identifies `sk_decode_sum` as relevant to the trigger path
- BUT: Incorrectly states that the trigger is `sk_decode_sum` returning -1 (failure), which does NOT actually reach the sink (it goes to exit_fail). The real trigger is `sk_decode_sum` SUCCEEDING but not populating all fields.
- Score: 5/10 - better data-flow understanding but wrong about the specific trigger condition

Actually, I want to reconsider. Do both explanations substantially answer the question? The baseline says "memset zeros, then code dereferences" - this is technically correct since memset IS the root cause (it creates the NULL pointers) and the code dereferences without check. The missing intermediate step (sk_decode_sum) might be considered too detailed for a root cause explanation.

VastDB tries to be more specific about the trigger path but gets it partially wrong.

I think:
- Baseline is correct but overly-simplified (score around 6)
- VastDB is partially wrong on a key detail (score around 5)

Let me settle on:
- Baseline: correct=false (incomplete trigger path), score=5
- VastDB: correct=false (wrong trigger condition), score=4

Actually, rethinking again... The CVE.json says the source is `memset(&oldsum, 0, sizeof(sk_sum_t));` and the sink is `if (!*newsum->md5 || !*oldsum->md5 || ...)`. This is a straightforward null-pointer-dereference caused by memset zeroing pointers combined with the code not checking for NULL before dereferencing.

Both explanations are essentially saying this. The baseline is more direct and simpler. The vastDB tries to add more specificity but adds an incorrect detail.

For "correct" I should evaluate whether the explanation accurately captures the root cause and trigger path.

The root cause is: stack-allocated `sk_sum_t` structs are zero-initialized via memset, setting pointer fields (`md5`, `sha1`, `sha256`) to NULL. These fields may not be populated by subsequent calls (e.g., `sk_decode_sum` may leave them NULL if the encoded checksum doesn't contain those hash values). The `fim_alert` function at line 510 dereferences these pointers with `!*newsum->md5` / `!*oldsum->md5` without any NULL check, causing a null pointer dereference.

Baseline says: memset zeros all pointers, fim_alert dereferences them without NULL check. Missing: that sk_decode_sum may leave them null.

VastDB says: memset zeros pointers, sk_decode_sum may return -1 leaving newsum NULL, then fim_alert dereferences. Wrong: if sk_decode_sum returns -1, fim_alert isn't called.

I'll go with my assessment.

Now I have a thorough understanding of the code. Here is my structural analysis:

**Fix:** In `good.c`, lines 510-511 and 522-523, the vulnerable pattern `!*ptr` is replaced by `ptr == NULL || ptr[0] == '\0'`, adding NULL checks before dereferencing. This confirms CWE-476 Null Pointer Dereference.

**Trigger mechanism:** `sk_sum_t` structs `oldsum`/`newsum` are zeroed via `memset` at lines 691-692. Then `sk_decode_sum()` is called to populate fields from an encoded checksum string. If the encoded string lacks a hash field (e.g., `md5`), that pointer field stays NULL. When `fim_alert` reaches line 510 (only for `FIM_MODIFIED`), `!*newsum->md5` dereferences the NULL pointer. Importantly, if `sk_decode_sum` *fails* (returns -1), the function exits via `goto exit_fail` at line 862 *before* calling `fim_alert`, so a failure return cannot reach the sink.

Based on my analysis, I have enough information. Let me now provide the final answer:

## Root Cause

The vulnerability is a Null Pointer Dereference (CWE-476) in the `fim_alert` function. The `sk_sum_t` structure contains pointer fields (`md5`, `sha1`, `sha256`, etc.). At line 691-692 in `fim_db_search`, `memset(&oldsum, 0, sizeof(sk_sum_t))` and `memset(&newsum, 0, sizeof(sk_sum_t))` zero out both structures, setting all pointer fields to NULL. While `oldsum` gets populated via `sk_decode_extradata(&oldsum, check_sum)` at line 724, `newsum` may remain with NULL pointers if `sk_decode_sum(&newsum, c_sum, w_sum)` at line 731 fails (returns -1). At line 510 inside `fim_alert`, the code directly dereferences `newsum->md5` via `!*newsum->md5` without checking for NULL first, triggering the null pointer dereference.

## Trigger Path

1. `bad.c:673` `fim_db_search` - Entry point function that declares `sk_sum_t oldsum` and `newsum` on stack
2. `bad.c:691-692` - `memset(&oldsum, 0, sizeof(sk_sum_t))` and `memset(&newsum, 0, sizeof(sk_sum_t))` zero out both structs, setting all pointers (including `md5`) to NULL
3. `bad.c:724` `sk_decode_extradata(&oldsum, check_sum)` - Populates `oldsum` from database
4. `bad.c:731` `sk_decode_sum(&newsum, c_sum, w_sum)` - Attempts to populate `newsum`; may fail and leave `newsum->md5` as NULL
5. `bad.c:874` `fim_alert(f_name, &oldsum, &newsum, lf, sdb, event_type)` - Called with potentially uninitialized `newsum`
6. `bad.c:510` inside `fim_alert` - The sink: `if (!*newsum->md5 || !*oldsum->md5 || strcmp(...))` dereferences `newsum->md5` without NULL check, triggering CWE-476

405: int fim_alert (char *f_name, sk_sum_t *oldsum, sk_sum_t *newsum, Eventinfo *lf, _sdb *localsdb, syscheck_event_t event_type) {
406:     int changes = 0;
407:     char msg_type[OS_FLSIZE];
408:     char buf_ptr[26];
409: 
410:     if (event_type == FIM_DELETED) {
411:         snprintf(msg_type, sizeof(msg_type), "was deleted.");
412:         lf->decoder_info->id = fim_decoders[FILE_DECODER]->delete_id;
413:         lf->decoder_syscheck_id = lf->decoder_info->id;
414:         lf->decoder_info->name = fim_decoders[FILE_DECODER]->delete_name;
415:         changes = 1;
416:     } else if (event_type == FIM_ADDED) {
417:         snprintf(msg_type, sizeof(msg_type), "was added.");
418:         lf->decoder_info->id = fim_decoders[FILE_DECODER]->add_id;
419:         lf->decoder_syscheck_id = lf->decoder_info->id;
420:         lf->decoder_info->name = fim_decoders[FILE_DECODER]->add_name;
421:         changes = 1;
422:     } else if (event_type == FIM_MODIFIED) {
423:         snprintf(msg_type, sizeof(msg_type), "checksum changed.");
424:         lf->decoder_info->id = fim_decoders[FILE_DECODER]->modify_id;
425:         lf->decoder_syscheck_id = lf->decoder_info->id;
426:         lf->decoder_info->name = fim_decoders[FILE_DECODER]->modify_name;
427:         if (oldsum->size && newsum->size) {
428:             if (strcmp(oldsum->size, newsum->size) == 0) {
429:                 localsdb->size[0] = '\0';
430:             } else {
431:                 changes = 1;
432:                 wm_strcat(&lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value, "size", ',');
433:                 snprintf(localsdb->size, OS_FLSIZE,
434:                             "Size changed from '%s' to '%s'\n",
435:                             oldsum->size, newsum->size);
436: 
437:                 os_strdup(oldsum->size, lf->fields[FIM_SIZE_BEFORE].value);
438:             }
439:         }
440: 
441:         /* Permission message */
442:         if (oldsum->perm && newsum->perm) {
443:             if (oldsum->perm == newsum->perm) {
444:                 localsdb->perm[0] = '\0';
445:             } else if (oldsum->perm > 0 && newsum->perm > 0) {
446:                 changes = 1;
447:                 wm_strcat(&lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value, "perm", ',');
448:                 char opstr[10];
449:                 char npstr[10];
450:                 lf->fields[FIM_PERM_BEFORE].value = agent_file_perm(oldsum->perm);
451:                 char *new_perm = agent_file_perm(newsum->perm);
452: 
453:                 strncpy(opstr, lf->fields[FIM_PERM_BEFORE].value, sizeof(opstr) - 1);
454:                 strncpy(npstr, new_perm, sizeof(npstr) - 1);
455:                 free(new_perm);
456: 
457:                 opstr[9] = npstr[9] = '\0';
458:                 snprintf(localsdb->perm, OS_FLSIZE, "Permissions changed from "
459:                             "'%9.9s' to '%9.9s'\n", opstr, npstr);
460:             }
461:         } else if (oldsum->win_perm && newsum->win_perm) { // Check for Windows permissions
462:             // We need to unescape the old permissions at this point
463:             char *unesc_perms = wstr_replace(oldsum->win_perm, "\\:", ":");
464:             free(oldsum->win_perm);
465:             oldsum->win_perm = unesc_perms;
466:             if (strcmp(oldsum->win_perm, newsum->win_perm) == 0) {
467:                 localsdb->perm[0] = '\0';
468:             } else if (*oldsum->win_perm != '\0' && *newsum->win_perm != '\0') {
469:                 changes = 1;
470:                 wm_strcat(&lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value, "perm", ',');
471:                 snprintf(localsdb->perm, OS_FLSIZE, "Permissions changed.\n");
472:                 os_strdup(oldsum->win_perm, lf->fields[FIM_PERM_BEFORE].value);
473:             }
474:         }
475: 
476:         /* Ownership message */
477:         if (newsum->uid && oldsum->uid) {
478:             if (strcmp(newsum->uid, oldsum->uid) == 0) {
479:                 localsdb->owner[0] = '\0';
480:             } else {
481:                 changes = 1;
482:                 wm_strcat(&lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value, "uid", ',');
483:                 if (oldsum->uname && newsum->uname) {
484:                     snprintf(localsdb->owner, OS_FLSIZE, "Ownership was '%s (%s)', now it is '%s (%s)'\n", oldsum->uname, oldsum->uid, newsum->uname, newsum->uid);
485:                     os_strdup(oldsum->uname, lf->fields[FIM_UNAME_BEFORE].value);
486:                 } else {
487:                     snprintf(localsdb->owner, OS_FLSIZE, "Ownership was '%s', now it is '%s'\n", oldsum->uid, newsum->uid);
488:                 }
489:                 os_strdup(oldsum->uid, lf->fields[FIM_UID_BEFORE].value);
490:             }
491:         }
492: 
493:         /* Group ownership message */
494:         if (newsum->gid && oldsum->gid) {
495:             if (strcmp(newsum->gid, oldsum->gid) == 0) {
496:                 localsdb->gowner[0] = '\0';
497:             } else {
498:                 changes = 1;
499:                 wm_strcat(&lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value, "gid", ',');
500:                 if (oldsum->gname && newsum->gname) {
501:                     snprintf(localsdb->gowner, OS_FLSIZE, "Group ownership was '%s (%s)', now it is '%s (%s)'\n", oldsum->gname, oldsum->gid, newsum->gname, newsum->gid);
502:                     os_strdup(oldsum->gname, lf->fields[FIM_GNAME_BEFORE].value);
503:                 } else {
504:                     snprintf(localsdb->gowner, OS_FLSIZE, "Group ownership was '%s', now it is '%s'\n", oldsum->gid, newsum->gid);
505:                 }
506:                 os_strdup(oldsum->gid, lf->fields[FIM_GID_BEFORE].value);
507:             }
508:         }
509:         /* MD5 message */
510:         if (!*newsum->md5 || !*oldsum->md5 || strcmp(newsum->md5, oldsum->md5) == 0) {
511:             localsdb->md5[0] = '\0';
512:         } else {
513:             changes = 1;
514:             wm_strcat(&lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value, "md5", ',');
515:             snprintf(localsdb->md5, OS_FLSIZE, "Old md5sum was: '%s'\nNew md5sum is : '%s'\n",
516:                         oldsum->md5, newsum->md5);
517:             os_strdup(oldsum->md5, lf->fields[FIM_MD5_BEFORE].value);
518:         }
519: 
520:         /* SHA-1 message */
521:         if (!*newsum->sha1 || !*oldsum->sha1 || strcmp(newsum->sha1, oldsum->sha1) == 0) {
522:             localsdb->sha1[0] = '\0';
523:         } else {
524:             changes = 1;
525:             wm_strcat(&lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value, "sha1", ',');
526:             snprintf(localsdb->sha1, OS_FLSIZE, "Old sha1sum was: '%s'\nNew sha1sum is : '%s'\n",
527:                         oldsum->sha1, newsum->sha1);
528:             os_strdup(oldsum->sha1, lf->fields[FIM_SHA1_BEFORE].value);
529:         }
530: 
531:         /* SHA-256 message */
532:         if(newsum->sha256 && newsum->sha256[0] != '\0') {
533:             if(oldsum->sha256) {
534:                 if (strcmp(newsum->sha256, oldsum->sha256) == 0) {
535:                     localsdb->sha256[0] = '\0';
536:                 } else {
537:                     changes = 1;
538:                     wm_strcat(&lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value, "sha256", ',');
539:                     snprintf(localsdb->sha256, OS_FLSIZE, "Old sha256sum was: '%s'\nNew sha256sum is : '%s'\n",
540:                             oldsum->sha256, newsum->sha256);
541:                     os_strdup(oldsum->sha256, lf->fields[FIM_SHA256_BEFORE].value);
542:                 }
543:             } else {
544:                 changes = 1;
545:                 wm_strcat(&lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value, "sha256", ',');
546:                 snprintf(localsdb->sha256, OS_FLSIZE, "New sha256sum is : '%s'\n", newsum->sha256);
547:             }
548:         } else {
549:             localsdb->sha256[0] = '\0';
550:         }
551: 
552:         /* Modification time message */
553:         if (oldsum->mtime && newsum->mtime && oldsum->mtime != newsum->mtime) {
554:             changes = 1;
555:             wm_strcat(&lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value, "mtime", ',');
556:             char *old_ctime = strdup(ctime_r(&oldsum->mtime, buf_ptr));
557:             char *new_ctime = strdup(ctime_r(&newsum->mtime, buf_ptr));
558:             old_ctime[strlen(old_ctime) - 1] = '\0';
559:             new_ctime[strlen(new_ctime) - 1] = '\0';
560: 
561:             snprintf(localsdb->mtime, OS_FLSIZE, "Old modification time was: '%s', now it is '%s'\n", old_ctime, new_ctime);
562:             lf->fields[FIM_MTIME_BEFORE].value = w_long_str(oldsum->mtime);
563:             os_free(old_ctime);
564:             os_free(new_ctime);
565:         } else {
566:             localsdb->mtime[0] = '\0';
567:         }
568: 
569:         /* Inode message */
570:         if (oldsum->inode && newsum->inode && oldsum->inode != newsum->inode) {
571:             changes = 1;
572:             wm_strcat(&lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value, "inode", ',');
573:             snprintf(localsdb->inode, OS_FLSIZE, "Old inode was: '%ld', now it is '%ld'\n", oldsum->inode, newsum->inode);
574:             lf->fields[FIM_INODE_BEFORE].value = w_long_str(oldsum->inode);
575:         } else {
576:             localsdb->inode[0] = '\0';
577:         }
578: 
579:         /* Attributes message */
580:         if (oldsum->attributes && newsum->attributes
581:             && strcmp(oldsum->attributes, newsum->attributes)) {
582:             changes = 1;
583:             wm_strcat(&lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value, "attributes", ',');
584:             snprintf(localsdb->attrs, OS_SIZE_1024, "Old attributes were: '%s'\nNow they are '%s'\n", oldsum->attributes, newsum->attributes);
585:             os_strdup(oldsum->attributes, lf->fields[FIM_ATTRS_BEFORE].value);
586:         } else {
587:             localsdb->attrs[0] = '\0';
588:         }
589:     } else {
590:         return (-1);
591:     }
592: 
593:     /* Symbolic path message */
594:     if (newsum->symbolic_path && *newsum->symbolic_path) {
595:         snprintf(localsdb->sym_path, OS_FLSIZE, "Symbolic path: '%s'.\n", newsum->symbolic_path);
596:     } else {
597:         *localsdb->sym_path = '\0';
598:     }
599: 
600:     // Provide information about the file
601:     snprintf(localsdb->comment, OS_MAXSTR, "File"
602:             " '%.756s' "
603:             "%s\n"
604:             "%s"
605:             "%s"
606:             "%s"
607:             "%s"
608:             "%s"
609:             "%s"
610:             "%s"
611:             "%s"
612:             "%s"
613:             "%s"
614:             "%s",
615:             f_name,
616:             msg_type,
617:             localsdb->sym_path,
618:             localsdb->size,
619:             localsdb->perm,
620:             localsdb->owner,
621:             localsdb->gowner,
622:             localsdb->md5,
623:             localsdb->sha1,
624:             localsdb->sha256,
625:             localsdb->attrs,
626:             localsdb->mtime,
627:             localsdb->inode
628:     );
629:     if(!changes) {
630:         os_free(lf->data);
631:         return(-1);
632:     } else if (lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value != NULL) {
633:         wm_strcat(&lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value, ",", '\0');
634:     }
635: 
636:     // Create a new log message
637:     free(lf->full_log);
638:     os_strdup(localsdb->comment, lf->full_log);
639:     lf->log = lf->full_log;
640: 
641:     return (0);
642: }

644: int sk_decode_extradata(sk_sum_t *sum, char *c_sum) {
645:     char *changes;
646:     char *date_alert;
647:     char *sym_path;
648: 
649:     assert(sum != NULL);
650:     assert(c_sum != NULL);
651: 
652:     if (changes = strchr(c_sum, '!'), !changes) {
653:         return 0;
654:     }
655:     *changes++ = '\0';
656: 
657:     if (date_alert = strchr(changes, ':'), !date_alert) {
658:         return 0;
659:     }
660:     *(date_alert++) = '\0';
661: 
662:     if (sym_path = strchr(date_alert, ':'), sym_path) {
663:         *(sym_path++) = '\0';
664:         sum->symbolic_path = unescape_syscheck_field(sym_path);
665:     }
666: 
667:     sum->changes = atoi(changes);
668:     sum->date_alert = atol(date_alert);
669: 
670:     return 1;
671: }

703: 
704:     switch (db_result) {
705:     case -2:
706:         merror("FIM decoder: Bad load query: '%s'.", wazuhdb_query);
707:         // Fallthrough
708:     case -1:
709:         os_free(lf->data);
710:         goto exit_fail;
711:     }
712: 
713:     if(check_sum = wstr_chr(response, ' '), !check_sum) {
714:         merror("FIM decoder: Bad response: '%s' '%s'.", wazuhdb_query, response);
715:         goto exit_fail;
716:     }
717:     *(check_sum++) = '\0';
718: 
719:     if (strcmp(response, "ok") != 0) {
720:         goto exit_fail;
721:     }
722: 
723:     //extract changes and date_alert fields only available from wazuh_db
724:     sk_decode_extradata(&oldsum, check_sum);
725: 
726:     os_strdup(check_sum, old_check_sum);
727:     mdebug2("Agent '%s' File '%s'", lf->agent_id, f_name);
728:     mdebug2("Agent '%s' Old checksum '%s'", lf->agent_id, old_check_sum);
729:     mdebug2("Agent '%s' New checksum '%s'", lf->agent_id, new_check_sum);
730: 
731:     if (decode_newsum = sk_decode_sum(&newsum, c_sum, w_sum), decode_newsum != -1) {
732:         InsertWhodata(&newsum, sdb);
733:     }
734: 
735:     fim_adjust_checksum(&newsum, &new_check_sum);
736: 
737:     // Checksum match, we can just return and keep going
738:     if (SumCompare(old_check_sum, new_check_sum) == 0) {
739:         mdebug1("Agent '%s' Alert discarded '%s' same check_sum", lf->agent_id, f_name);
740:         fim_update_date (f_name, lf, sdb);
741:         goto exit_ok;
742:     }
743: 
744:     wazuhdb_query[0] = '\0';
745:     switch (decode_newsum) {
746:         case 1: // File deleted
747:             os_strdup(SYSCHECK_EVENT_STRINGS[FIM_DELETED], lf->fields[FIM_EVENT_TYPE].value);
748:             event_type = FIM_DELETED;
749: 
750:             if(!*old_check_sum){
751:                 mdebug2("Agent '%s' Alert already reported (double delete alert)", lf->agent_id);
752:                 goto exit_ok;
753:             }
754: 
755:             snprintf(wazuhdb_query, OS_SIZE_6144, "agent %s syscheck delete %s",
756:                     lf->agent_id,
757:                     f_name
758:             );
759: 
760:             db_result = wdbc_query_ex(&sdb->socket, wazuhdb_query, response, OS_SIZE_6144);
761: 
762:             switch (db_result) {
763:             case -2:

766:             case -1:
767:                 goto exit_fail;
768:             }
769: 
770:             mdebug2("Agent '%s' File %s deleted from FIM DDBB", lf->agent_id, f_name);
771: 
772:             break;
773:         case 0:
774:             if (*old_check_sum) {
775:                 // File modified
776:                 os_strdup(SYSCHECK_EVENT_STRINGS[FIM_MODIFIED], lf->fields[FIM_EVENT_TYPE].value);
777:                 event_type = FIM_MODIFIED;
778:                 changes = fim_check_changes(oldsum.changes, oldsum.date_alert, lf);
779:                 sk_decode_sum(&oldsum, old_check_sum, NULL);
780: 
781:                 // Alert discarded, frequency exceeded
782:                 if (changes == -1) {
783:                     mdebug1("Agent '%s' Alert discarded '%s' frequency exceeded", lf->agent_id, f_name);
784:                     goto exit_ok;
785:                 }
786:             } else {
787:                 // File added
788:                 os_strdup(SYSCHECK_EVENT_STRINGS[FIM_ADDED], lf->fields[FIM_EVENT_TYPE].value);
789:                 event_type = FIM_ADDED;
790:             }
791: 
792:             if (strstr(lf->location, "syscheck-registry")) {
793:                 *ttype = "registry";
794:             } else {
795:                 *ttype = "file";
796:             }
797: 
798:             if (newsum.symbolic_path) {

815:             os_free(checksum_esc);
816:             db_result = wdbc_query_ex(&sdb->socket, wazuhdb_query, response, OS_SIZE_6144);

818:             switch (db_result) {
819:             case -2:
820:                 merror("FIM decoder: Bad save/update query: '%s'.", wazuhdb_query);
821:                 // Fallthrough
822:             case -1:
823:                 goto exit_fail;
824:             }
825: 
826:             mdebug2("Agent '%s' File %s saved/updated in FIM DDBB", lf->agent_id, f_name);
827: 
828:             if (end_first_scan = (time_t *)OSHash_Get_ex(fim_agentinfo, lf->agent_id), end_first_scan == NULL) {
829:                 fim_get_scantime(&end_scan, lf, sdb, "end_scan");
830:                 os_calloc(1, sizeof(time_t), end_first_scan);
831:                 *end_first_scan = end_scan;
832:                 int res;
833:                 if (res = OSHash_Add_ex(fim_agentinfo, lf->agent_id, end_first_scan), res != 2) {
834:                     os_free(end_first_scan);
835:                     if (res == 0) {
836:                         merror("Unable to add scan_info to hash table for agent: %s", lf->agent_id);
837:                     }
838:                 }
839:             } else {
840:                 end_scan = *end_first_scan;
841:             }
842: 
843:             if (event_type == FIM_ADDED) {
844:                 if (end_scan == 0) {
845:                     mdebug2("Agent '%s' Alert discarded, first scan. File '%s'", lf->agent_id, f_name);
846:                     goto exit_ok;
847:                 } else if (lf->time.tv_sec < end_scan) {
848:                     mdebug2("Agent '%s' Alert discarded, first scan (delayed event). File '%s'", lf->agent_id, f_name);
849:                     goto exit_ok;
850:                 } else if (Config.syscheck_alert_new == 0) {
851:                     mdebug2("Agent '%s' Alert discarded (alert_new_files = no). File '%s'", lf->agent_id, f_name);
852:                     goto exit_ok;
853:                 }
854:             }
855: 
856:             mdebug2("Agent '%s' End end_scan is '%ld' (lf->time: '%ld')", lf->agent_id, end_scan, lf->time.tv_sec);
857:             break;
858: 
859:         default: // Error in fim check sum
860:             mwarn("at fim_db_search: Agent '%s' Couldn't decode fim sum '%s' from file '%s'.",
861:                     lf->agent_id, new_check_sum, f_name);
862:             goto exit_fail;
863:     }
864: 
865:     if (!newsum.silent) {
866:         sk_fill_event(lf, f_name, &newsum);
867: 
868:         /* Dyanmic Fields */
869:         lf->nfields = FIM_NFIELDS;

892:     sk_sum_clean(&oldsum);

405: int fim_alert (char *f_name, sk_sum_t *oldsum, sk_sum_t *newsum, Eventinfo *lf, _sdb *localsdb, syscheck_event_t event_type) {
406:     int changes = 0;
407:     char msg_type[OS_FLSIZE];
408:     char buf_ptr[26];
409: 
410:     if (event_type == FIM_DELETED) {
411:         snprintf(msg_type, sizeof(msg_type), "was deleted.");
412:         lf->decoder_info->id = fim_decoders[FILE_DECODER]->delete_id;
413:         lf->decoder_syscheck_id = lf->decoder_info->id;
414:         lf->decoder_info->name = fim_decoders[FILE_DECODER]->delete_name;
415:         changes = 1;
416:     } else if (event_type == FIM_ADDED) {
417:         snprintf(msg_type, sizeof(msg_type), "was added.");
418:         lf->decoder_info->id = fim_decoders[FILE_DECODER]->add_id;
419:         lf->decoder_syscheck_id = lf->decoder_info->id;
420:         lf->decoder_info->name = fim_decoders[FILE_DECODER]->add_name;
421:         changes = 1;
422:     } else if (event_type == FIM_MODIFIED) {
423:         snprintf(msg_type, sizeof(msg_type), "checksum changed.");
424:         lf->decoder_info->id = fim_decoders[FILE_DECODER]->modify_id;
425:         lf->decoder_syscheck_id = lf->decoder_info->id;
426:         lf->decoder_info->name = fim_decoders[FILE_DECODER]->modify_name;
427:         if (oldsum->size && newsum->size) {
428:             if (strcmp(oldsum->size, newsum->size) == 0) {
429:                 localsdb->size[0] = '\0';
430:             } else {
431:                 changes = 1;
432:                 wm_strcat(&lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value, "size", ',');
433:                 snprintf(localsdb->size, OS_FLSIZE,
434:                             "Size changed from '%s' to '%s'\n",
435:                             oldsum->size, newsum->size);
436: 
437:                 os_strdup(oldsum->size, lf->fields[FIM_SIZE_BEFORE].value);
438:             }
439:         }
440: 
441:         /* Permission message */
442:         if (oldsum->perm && newsum->perm) {
443:             if (oldsum->perm == newsum->perm) {
444:                 localsdb->perm[0] = '\0';
445:             } else if (oldsum->perm > 0 && newsum->perm > 0) {
446:                 changes = 1;
447:                 wm_strcat(&lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value, "perm", ',');
448:                 char opstr[10];
449:                 char npstr[10];
450:                 lf->fields[FIM_PERM_BEFORE].value = agent_file_perm(oldsum->perm);
451:                 char *new_perm = agent_file_perm(newsum->perm);
452: 
453:                 strncpy(opstr, lf->fields[FIM_PERM_BEFORE].value, sizeof(opstr) - 1);
454:                 strncpy(npstr, new_perm, sizeof(npstr) - 1);
455:                 free(new_perm);
456: 
457:                 opstr[9] = npstr[9] = '\0';
458:                 snprintf(localsdb->perm, OS_FLSIZE, "Permissions changed from "
459:                             "'%9.9s' to '%9.9s'\n", opstr, npstr);
460:             }
461:         } else if (oldsum->win_perm && newsum->win_perm) { // Check for Windows permissions
462:             // We need to unescape the old permissions at this point
463:             char *unesc_perms = wstr_replace(oldsum->win_perm, "\\:", ":");
464:             free(oldsum->win_perm);
465:             oldsum->win_perm = unesc_perms;
466:             if (strcmp(oldsum->win_perm, newsum->win_perm) == 0) {
467:                 localsdb->perm[0] = '\0';
468:             } else if (*oldsum->win_perm != '\0' && *newsum->win_perm != '\0') {
469:                 changes = 1;
470:                 wm_strcat(&lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value, "perm", ',');
471:                 snprintf(localsdb->perm, OS_FLSIZE, "Permissions changed.\n");
472:                 os_strdup(oldsum->win_perm, lf->fields[FIM_PERM_BEFORE].value);
473:             }
474:         }
475: 
476:         /* Ownership message */
477:         if (newsum->uid && oldsum->uid) {
478:             if (strcmp(newsum->uid, oldsum->uid) == 0) {
479:                 localsdb->owner[0] = '\0';
480:             } else {
481:                 changes = 1;
482:                 wm_strcat(&lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value, "uid", ',');
483:                 if (oldsum->uname && newsum->uname) {
484:                     snprintf(localsdb->owner, OS_FLSIZE, "Ownership was '%s (%s)', now it is '%s (%s)'\n", oldsum->uname, oldsum->uid, newsum->uname, newsum->uid);
485:                     os_strdup(oldsum->uname, lf->fields[FIM_UNAME_BEFORE].value);
486:                 } else {
487:                     snprintf(localsdb->owner, OS_FLSIZE, "Ownership was '%s', now it is '%s'\n", oldsum->uid, newsum->uid);
488:                 }
489:                 os_strdup(oldsum->uid, lf->fields[FIM_UID_BEFORE].value);
490:             }
491:         }
492: 
493:         /* Group ownership message */
494:         if (newsum->gid && oldsum->gid) {
495:             if (strcmp(newsum->gid, oldsum->gid) == 0) {
496:                 localsdb->gowner[0] = '\0';
497:             } else {
498:                 changes = 1;
499:                 wm_strcat(&lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value, "gid", ',');
500:                 if (oldsum->gname && newsum->gname) {
501:                     snprintf(localsdb->gowner, OS_FLSIZE, "Group ownership was '%s (%s)', now it is '%s (%s)'\n", oldsum->gname, oldsum->gid, newsum->gname, newsum->gid);
502:                     os_strdup(oldsum->gname, lf->fields[FIM_GNAME_BEFORE].value);
503:                 } else {
504:                     snprintf(localsdb->gowner, OS_FLSIZE, "Group ownership was '%s', now it is '%s'\n", oldsum->gid, newsum->gid);
505:                 }
506:                 os_strdup(oldsum->gid, lf->fields[FIM_GID_BEFORE].value);
507:             }
508:         }
509:         /* MD5 message */
510:         if (newsum->md5 == NULL || newsum->md5[0] == '\0' || oldsum->md5 == NULL || oldsum->md5[0] == '\0'
511:             || strcmp(newsum->md5, oldsum->md5) == 0) {
512:             localsdb->md5[0] = '\0';
513:         } else {
514:             changes = 1;
515:             wm_strcat(&lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value, "md5", ',');
516:             snprintf(localsdb->md5, OS_FLSIZE, "Old md5sum was: '%s'\nNew md5sum is : '%s'\n",
517:                         oldsum->md5, newsum->md5);
518:             os_strdup(oldsum->md5, lf->fields[FIM_MD5_BEFORE].value);
519:         }
520: 
521:         /* SHA-1 message */
522:         if (newsum->sha1 == NULL || newsum->sha1[0] == '\0' || oldsum->sha1 == NULL || oldsum->sha1[0] == '\0'
523:             || strcmp(newsum->sha1, oldsum->sha1) == 0) {
524:             localsdb->sha1[0] = '\0';
525:         } else {
526:             changes = 1;
527:             wm_strcat(&lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value, "sha1", ',');
528:             snprintf(localsdb->sha1, OS_FLSIZE, "Old sha1sum was: '%s'\nNew sha1sum is : '%s'\n",
529:                         oldsum->sha1, newsum->sha1);
530:             os_strdup(oldsum->sha1, lf->fields[FIM_SHA1_BEFORE].value);
531:         }
532: 
533:         /* SHA-256 message */
534:         if(newsum->sha256 && newsum->sha256[0] != '\0') {
535:             if(oldsum->sha256) {
536:                 if (strcmp(newsum->sha256, oldsum->sha256) == 0) {
537:                     localsdb->sha256[0] = '\0';
538:                 } else {
539:                     changes = 1;
540:                     wm_strcat(&lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value, "sha256", ',');
541:                     snprintf(localsdb->sha256, OS_FLSIZE, "Old sha256sum was: '%s'\nNew sha256sum is : '%s'\n",
542:                             oldsum->sha256, newsum->sha256);
543:                     os_strdup(oldsum->sha256, lf->fields[FIM_SHA256_BEFORE].value);
544:                 }
545:             } else {
546:                 changes = 1;
547:                 wm_strcat(&lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value, "sha256", ',');
548:                 snprintf(localsdb->sha256, OS_FLSIZE, "New sha256sum is : '%s'\n", newsum->sha256);
549:             }
550:         } else {
551:             localsdb->sha256[0] = '\0';
552:         }
553: 
554:         /* Modification time message */
555:         if (oldsum->mtime && newsum->mtime && oldsum->mtime != newsum->mtime) {
556:             changes = 1;
557:             wm_strcat(&lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value, "mtime", ',');
558:             char *old_ctime = strdup(ctime_r(&oldsum->mtime, buf_ptr));
559:             char *new_ctime = strdup(ctime_r(&newsum->mtime, buf_ptr));
560:             old_ctime[strlen(old_ctime) - 1] = '\0';
561:             new_ctime[strlen(new_ctime) - 1] = '\0';
562: 
563:             snprintf(localsdb->mtime, OS_FLSIZE, "Old modification time was: '%s', now it is '%s'\n", old_ctime, new_ctime);
564:             lf->fields[FIM_MTIME_BEFORE].value = w_long_str(oldsum->mtime);
565:             os_free(old_ctime);
566:             os_free(new_ctime);
567:         } else {
568:             localsdb->mtime[0] = '\0';
569:         }
570: 
571:         /* Inode message */
572:         if (oldsum->inode && newsum->inode && oldsum->inode != newsum->inode) {
573:             changes = 1;
574:             wm_strcat(&lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value, "inode", ',');
575:             snprintf(localsdb->inode, OS_FLSIZE, "Old inode was: '%ld', now it is '%ld'\n", oldsum->inode, newsum->inode);
576:             lf->fields[FIM_INODE_BEFORE].value = w_long_str(oldsum->inode);
577:         } else {
578:             localsdb->inode[0] = '\0';
579:         }
580: 
581:         /* Attributes message */
582:         if (oldsum->attributes && newsum->attributes
583:             && strcmp(oldsum->attributes, newsum->attributes)) {
584:             changes = 1;
585:             wm_strcat(&lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value, "attributes", ',');
586:             snprintf(localsdb->attrs, OS_SIZE_1024, "Old attributes were: '%s'\nNow they are '%s'\n", oldsum->attributes, newsum->attributes);
587:             os_strdup(oldsum->attributes, lf->fields[FIM_ATTRS_BEFORE].value);
588:         } else {
589:             localsdb->attrs[0] = '\0';
590:         }
591:     } else {
592:         return (-1);
593:     }
594: 
595:     /* Symbolic path message */
596:     if (newsum->symbolic_path && *newsum->symbolic_path) {
597:         snprintf(localsdb->sym_path, OS_FLSIZE, "Symbolic path: '%s'.\n", newsum->symbolic_path);
598:     } else {
599:         *localsdb->sym_path = '\0';
600:     }
601: 
602:     // Provide information about the file
603:     snprintf(localsdb->comment, OS_MAXSTR, "File"
604:             " '%.756s' "
605:             "%s\n"
606:             "%s"
607:             "%s"
608:             "%s"
609:             "%s"
610:             "%s"
611:             "%s"
612:             "%s"
613:             "%s"
614:             "%s"
615:             "%s"
616:             "%s",
617:             f_name,
618:             msg_type,
619:             localsdb->sym_path,
620:             localsdb->size,
621:             localsdb->perm,
622:             localsdb->owner,
623:             localsdb->gowner,
624:             localsdb->md5,
625:             localsdb->sha1,
626:             localsdb->sha256,
627:             localsdb->attrs,
628:             localsdb->mtime,
629:             localsdb->inode
630:     );
631:     if(!changes) {
632:         os_free(lf->data);
633:         return(-1);
634:     } else if (lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value != NULL) {
635:         wm_strcat(&lf->fields[FIM_CHFIELDS].value, ",", '\0');
636:     }
637: 
638:     // Create a new log message
639:     free(lf->full_log);
640:     os_strdup(localsdb->comment, lf->full_log);
641:     lf->log = lf->full_log;
642:     // Force clean event
643:     lf->program_name = NULL;
644:     lf->dec_timestamp = NULL;
645: 
646:     return (0);
647: }

649: int sk_decode_extradata(sk_sum_t *sum, char *c_sum) {
650:     char *changes;
651:     char *date_alert;
652:     char *sym_path;
653: 
654:     assert(sum != NULL);
655:     assert(c_sum != NULL);
656: 
657:     if (changes = strchr(c_sum, '!'), !changes) {
658:         return 0;
659:     }
660:     *changes++ = '\0';
661: 
662:     if (date_alert = strchr(changes, ':'), !date_alert) {
663:         return 0;
664:     }
665:     *(date_alert++) = '\0';
666: 
667:     if (sym_path = strchr(date_alert, ':'), sym_path) {
668:         *(sym_path++) = '\0';
669:         sum->symbolic_path = unescape_syscheck_field(sym_path);
670:     }
671: 
672:     sum->changes = atoi(changes);
673:     sum->date_alert = atol(date_alert);
674: 
675:     return 1;
676: }

678: int fim_db_search(char *f_name, char *c_sum, char *w_sum, Eventinfo *lf, _sdb *sdb) {
679:     int decode_newsum = 0;
680:     int db_result = 0;
681:     int changes = 0;
682:     int i = 0;
683:     char *ttype[OS_SIZE_128];
684:     char *wazuhdb_query = NULL;
685:     char *new_check_sum = NULL;
686:     char *old_check_sum = NULL;
687:     char *response = NULL;
688:     char *check_sum = NULL;
689:     char *sym_path = NULL;
690:     sk_sum_t oldsum = { .size = NULL };
691:     sk_sum_t newsum = { .size = NULL };
692:     time_t *end_first_scan = NULL;
693:     time_t end_scan = 0;
694:     syscheck_event_t event_type;
695: 
696:     memset(&oldsum, 0, sizeof(sk_sum_t));
697:     memset(&newsum, 0, sizeof(sk_sum_t));
698: 
699:     os_calloc(OS_SIZE_6144 + 1, sizeof(char), wazuhdb_query);
700:     os_strdup(c_sum, new_check_sum);
701: 
702:     snprintf(wazuhdb_query, OS_SIZE_6144, "agent %s syscheck load %s", lf->agent_id, f_name);
703: 
704:     os_calloc(OS_SIZE_6144, sizeof(char), response);
705:     db_result = wdbc_query_ex(&sdb->socket, wazuhdb_query, response, OS_SIZE_6144);
706: 
707:     // Fail trying load info from DDBB
708: 
709:     switch (db_result) {
710:     case -2:
711:         merror("FIM decoder: Bad load query: '%s'.", wazuhdb_query);
712:         // Fallthrough
713:     case -1:
714:         os_free(lf->data);
715:         goto exit_fail;
716:     }
717: 
718:     if(check_sum = wstr_chr(response, ' '), !check_sum) {
719:         merror("FIM decoder: Bad response: '%s' '%s'.", wazuhdb_query, response);
720:         goto exit_fail;
721:     }
722:     *(check_sum++) = '\0';
723: 
724:     if (strcmp(response, "ok") != 0) {
725:         goto exit_fail;
726:     }
727: 
728:     //extract changes and date_alert fields only available from wazuh_db
729:     sk_decode_extradata(&oldsum, check_sum);
730: 
731:     os_strdup(check_sum, old_check_sum);
732:     mdebug2("Agent '%s' File '%s'", lf->agent_id, f_name);
733:     mdebug2("Agent '%s' Old checksum '%s'", lf->agent_id, old_check_sum);
734:     mdebug2("Agent '%s' New checksum '%s'", lf->agent_id, new_check_sum);
735: 
736:     if (decode_newsum = sk_decode_sum(&newsum, c_sum, w_sum), decode_newsum != -1) {
737:         InsertWhodata(&newsum, sdb);
738:     }
739: 
740:     fim_adjust_checksum(&newsum, &new_check_sum);
741: 
742:     // Checksum match, we can just return and keep going
743:     if (SumCompare(old_check_sum, new_check_sum) == 0) {
744:         mdebug1("Agent '%s' Alert discarded '%s' same check_sum", lf->agent_id, f_name);
745:         fim_update_date (f_name, lf, sdb);
746:         goto exit_ok;
747:     }
748: 
749:     wazuhdb_query[0] = '\0';
750:     switch (decode_newsum) {
751:         case 1: // File deleted
752:             os_strdup(SYSCHECK_EVENT_STRINGS[FIM_DELETED], lf->fields[FIM_EVENT_TYPE].value);
753:             event_type = FIM_DELETED;
754: 
755:             if(!*old_check_sum){
756:                 mdebug2("Agent '%s' Alert already reported (double delete alert)", lf->agent_id);
757:                 goto exit_ok;
758:             }
759: 
760:             snprintf(wazuhdb_query, OS_SIZE_6144, "agent %s syscheck delete %s",
761:                     lf->agent_id,
762:                     f_name
763:             );
764: 
765:             db_result = wdbc_query_ex(&sdb->socket, wazuhdb_query, response, OS_SIZE_6144);
766: 
767:             switch (db_result) {
768:             case -2:
769:                 merror("FIM decoder: Bad delete query: '%s'.", wazuhdb_query);
770:                 // Fallthrough
771:             case -1:
772:                 goto exit_fail;
773:             }
774: 
775:             mdebug2("Agent '%s' File %s deleted from FIM DDBB", lf->agent_id, f_name);
776: 
777:             break;
778:         case 0:
779:             if (*old_check_sum) {
780:                 // File modified
781:                 os_strdup(SYSCHECK_EVENT_STRINGS[FIM_MODIFIED], lf->fields[FIM_EVENT_TYPE].value);
782:                 event_type = FIM_MODIFIED;
783:                 changes = fim_check_changes(oldsum.changes, oldsum.date_alert, lf);
784:                 sk_decode_sum(&oldsum, old_check_sum, NULL);
785: 
786:                 // Alert discarded, frequency exceeded
787:                 if (changes == -1) {
788:                     mdebug1("Agent '%s' Alert discarded '%s' frequency exceeded", lf->agent_id, f_name);
789:                     goto exit_ok;
790:                 }
791:             } else {
792:                 // File added
793:                 os_strdup(SYSCHECK_EVENT_STRINGS[FIM_ADDED], lf->fields[FIM_EVENT_TYPE].value);
794:                 event_type = FIM_ADDED;
795:             }
796: 
797:             if (strstr(lf->location, "syscheck-registry")) {
798:                 *ttype = "registry";
799:             } else {
800:                 *ttype = "file";
801:             }
802: 
803:             if (newsum.symbolic_path) {
804:                 sym_path = escape_syscheck_field(newsum.symbolic_path);
805:             }
806: 
807:             // We need to escape the checksum because it will have
808:             // spaces if the event comes from Windows
809:             char *checksum_esc = wstr_replace(new_check_sum, " ", "\\ ");
810:             snprintf(wazuhdb_query, OS_SIZE_6144, "agent %s syscheck save %s %s!%d:%ld:%s %s",
811:                     lf->agent_id,
812:                     *ttype,
813:                     checksum_esc,
814:                     changes,
815:                     lf->time.tv_sec,
816:                     sym_path ? sym_path : "",
817:                     f_name
818:             );
819:             os_free(sym_path);
820:             os_free(checksum_esc);
821:             db_result = wdbc_query_ex(&sdb->socket, wazuhdb_query, response, OS_SIZE_6144);
822: 
823:             switch (db_result) {
824:             case -2:
825:                 merror("FIM decoder: Bad save/update query: '%s'.", wazuhdb_query);
826:                 // Fallthrough
827:             case -1:
828:                 goto exit_fail;
829:             }
830: 
831:             mdebug2("Agent '%s' File %s saved/updated in FIM DDBB", lf->agent_id, f_name);
832: 
833:             if (end_first_scan = (time_t *)OSHash_Get_ex(fim_agentinfo, lf->agent_id), end_first_scan == NULL) {
834:                 fim_get_scantime(&end_scan, lf, sdb, "end_scan");
835:                 os_calloc(1, sizeof(time_t), end_first_scan);
836:                 *end_first_scan = end_scan;
837:                 int res;
838:                 if (res = OSHash_Add_ex(fim_agentinfo, lf->agent_id, end_first_scan), res != 2) {
839:                     os_free(end_first_scan);
840:                     if (res == 0) {
841:                         merror("Unable to add scan_info to hash table for agent: %s", lf->agent_id);
842:                     }
843:                 }
844:             } else {
845:                 end_scan = *end_first_scan;
846:             }
847: 
848:             if (event_type == FIM_ADDED) {
849:                 if (end_scan == 0) {
850:                     mdebug2("Agent '%s' Alert discarded, first scan. File '%s'", lf->agent_id, f_name);
851:                     goto exit_ok;
852:                 } else if (lf->time.tv_sec < end_scan) {
853:                     mdebug2("Agent '%s' Alert discarded, first scan (delayed event). File '%s'", lf->agent_id, f_name);
854:                     goto exit_ok;
855:                 } else if (Config.syscheck_alert_new == 0) {
856:                     mdebug2("Agent '%s' Alert discarded (alert_new_files = no). File '%s'", lf->agent_id, f_name);
857:                     goto exit_ok;
858:                 }
859:             }
860: 
861:             mdebug2("Agent '%s' End end_scan is '%ld' (lf->time: '%ld')", lf->agent_id, end_scan, lf->time.tv_sec);
862:             break;
863: 
864:         default: // Error in fim check sum
865:             mwarn("at fim_db_search: Agent '%s' Couldn't decode fim sum '%s' from file '%s'.",
866:                     lf->agent_id, new_check_sum, f_name);
867:             goto exit_fail;
868:     }
869: 
870:     if (!newsum.silent) {
871:         sk_fill_event(lf, f_name, &newsum);
872:
